tasks.description: Add token budget calculation utility for MiniMax buffer
tasks.context: Create calculateTokenBudget() function that reduces max_tokens by 20% for MiniMax model. Input: model name, base limit. Output: adjusted limit. Place in backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts near existing model config. This enables surgical fix without touching main orchestration logic.

tasks.description: Apply 20% token buffer to MiniMax backend-core requests
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts, modify the agent dispatch logic to apply calculateTokenBudget() when routing to MiniMax for backend-core agent. Reduce from ~4500 to ~3600 tokens. Only apply to coding tasks (type: feature|bugfix). Leave frontend and test tasks unchanged.

tasks.description: Add truncation detection to orchestrator response handler
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts response processing, detect MiniMax truncation markers (incomplete code blocks, mid-line cuts). Log warning with task ID and token usage. Return structured error to caller instead of silently failing. This prevents future auto-approval issues.

tasks.description: Expose token budget metrics in orchestrator config
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/orchestrator-config.ts, add tokenBudgetMultiplier field (default 0.8 for 20% buffer). Export getEffectiveTokenLimit(agent, model) helper. This centralizes buffer logic and allows future tuning without code changes.

tasks.description: Add integration test for MiniMax token buffer enforcement
tasks.context: Create test in backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts (inline test block) that verifies: 1) MiniMax requests use reduced token limit, 2) Truncation detection triggers on incomplete output, 3) Buffer does not apply to frontend tasks. Mock MiniMax API response. Validates Week-9 fix.
